title: QUIC client-side 1-RTT Key Phase observation architecture
purpose: Describe how the managed client/runtime path records the first observed 1-RTT Key Phase transition on the active short-header packet path after handshake confirmation without successor-key derivation or public key-update support.
risks: If the short-header formatter and opener diverge on the Key Phase bit, the runtime can miss or misclassify the first observed transition.
If the runtime installs the transition before handshake confirmation, the slice overclaims a state boundary it does not yet own.
If successor-key derivation is added here, the slice becomes a broader RFC 9001 key-update implementation instead of a narrow observation seam.
design_summary: The 1-RTT short-header packet helper formats the Key Phase bit into the protected packet and reports the observed bit when opening the packet. The active client runtime receives the protected packet after handshake confirmation, observes the Key Phase bit from the opened application packet, and installs the first 0->1 transition by updating the existing bridge-state fields. The establishing runtime path is left unchanged, and the slice stops at the first observation instead of attempting successor-key derivation or broader RFC 9001 key-update handling.
alternatives_considered: Treat Key Phase as parser-only metadata. Rejected because the runtime needs to remember the first observed transition in existing bridge state.
Implement full successor-key derivation in the same slice. Rejected because that would overclaim the current boundary and widen the proof burden.
Expose a new public key-update API. Rejected because the slice must remain internal and managed-client only.
edge_cases_and_constraints: Establishing runtimes remain unchanged before handshake confirmation.
The slice records only the first observed 0->1 transition and does not derive successor key material.
The slice does not add TLS KeyUpdate support, transfer enablement, retry enablement, public API widening, or any public key-update promise.
